title: Top Five Biopics and the Brilliance of ‘Judas and the Black Messiah’
format: Sean and Amanda go through the Oscar shortlist, argue about what makes a good biopic, and each name five favorites, then Sean talks to Shaka King about writing and directing ‘Judas and the Black Messiah’.

segments.heading: The documentary and international feature shortlists

films.title: Dick Johnson Is Dead
films.note: Documentary shortlist.

films.title: Crip Camp
films.note: Documentary shortlist.

films.title: Boys State
films.note: Documentary shortlist.

films.title: Time
films.note: Documentary shortlist.

films.title: Another Round
films.note: International feature shortlist, Thomas Vinterberg directing Mads Mikkelsen. Amanda still hadn't seen it.

films.title: La Llorona
films.note: International feature shortlist, the Guatemalan film streaming on Shudder.

films.title: Dear Comrades!
films.note: International feature shortlist, the Russian entry.

films.title: A Sun
films.note: International feature shortlist, the Taiwanese entry, streaming on Netflix.

films.title: Honeyland
films.note: Not this year's shortlist, but the comparison point: last year's Macedonian beekeeper documentary that landed nods in both documentary and international feature.

segments.heading: The original song and score shortlists

films.title: Borat Subsequent Moviefilm
films.note: Its end-credits song, 'Wuhan Flu,' made the original song shortlist.

films.title: Eurovision Song Contest: The Story of Fire Saga
films.note: 'Husavik' was last year's shortlisted example of the category doing something weird.

films.title: A Star Is Born
films.note: Sean's example of a song actually written into the plot: 'Shallow.'

films.title: Belly of the Beast
films.note: Original song shortlist; neither host had heard of the film.

films.title: Giving Voice
films.note: Original song shortlist, for 'Never Break'; same complaint.

films.title: Judas and the Black Messiah
films.note: Its song also made the shortlist; the film gets its own segment below.

films.title: Mank
films.note: Original score shortlist, one of two Trent Reznor/Atticus Ross nominations.

films.title: Soul
films.note: Original score shortlist, the other Reznor/Ross nomination.

films.title: Tenet
films.note: Original score shortlist, Ludwig Göransson.

films.title: Minari
films.note: Original score shortlist, Emile Mosseri.

films.title: The Last Black Man in San Francisco
films.note: Mosseri's earlier score, which Sean calls one of the best of the last five years.

films.title: Gandhi
films.note: Their example of the airless 'great man' biopic: Richard Attenborough's film, a Ben Kingsley Oscar, and, in Amanda's words, 'kind of boring.'

films.title: Malcolm X
films.note: The exception that proves the rule: a cradle-to-grave biopic that still works. Left off both of their five-favorites lists because they'd covered it recently on the show.

segments.heading: Five favorite biopics
segments.note: each picked five, not strictly ranked

picks.title: Amadeus
picks.note: Milos Forman's Mozart/Salieri film, told through Salieri's jealousy. Tom Hulce plays Mozart as 'a little maniac,' nothing like the solemn historical bust.

picks.title: Julie & Julia
picks.note: Nora Ephron's film splits between Julia Child in Paris and a blogger cooking through her cookbook; Meryl Streep's performance goes past impersonation.

picks.title: Catch Me If You Can
picks.note: Spielberg's Frank Abagnale film, a con-man story about someone unfamous, and Sean's pick for best Leo DiCaprio biopic.

picks.title: The Queen
picks.note: Stephen Frears and Peter Morgan's film, Helen Mirren as Elizabeth II in the week after Princess Diana's death. Peter Morgan later expanded the same source material into 'The Crown.'

picks.title: The Elephant Man
picks.note: David Lynch's John Hurt/Anthony Hopkins film about Joseph Merrick, shot in black and white: a hushed, classically staged outlier in Lynch's filmography.

picks.title: Mishima: A Life in Four Chapters
picks.note: Paul Schrader's Yukio Mishima film, built from dramatized chapters of Mishima's own fiction.

picks.title: Love & Mercy
picks.note: Brian Wilson split across two eras and two actors, Paul Dano and John Cusack, with no attempt at physical continuity between them.

picks.title: Jackie
picks.note: Pablo Larraín's film, Natalie Portman as Jackie Kennedy in the days after JFK's assassination: more about image-making than impersonation.

picks.title: 32 Short Films About Glenn Gould
picks.note: François Girard's patchwork portrait of the pianist, structured after Gould's own Goldberg Variations recording. Amanda's dad showed it to her at age nine.

picks.title: I'm Not There
picks.note: Todd Haynes splits Bob Dylan into seven different performers and personas.

films.title: Judas and the Black Messiah
films.note: Shaka King's Fred Hampton film, out on HBO Max. LaKeith Stanfield plays FBI informant William O'Neill (the Judas of the title), Daniel Kaluuya plays Hampton, Dominique Fishback plays Deborah Johnson (Akua Njeri), and Jesse Plemons plays O'Neill's handler, Roy Mitchell. Martin Sheen plays J. Edgar Hoover. Sean and Amanda call it a Trojan-horse biopic: a crime thriller and cat-and-mouse movie about a socialist revolution, funded by Warner Bros.

films.title: MLK/FBI
films.note: Sam Pollard's documentary, also on this year's shortlist. Sean recommends pairing it with 'Judas and the Black Messiah' as a double feature, for more on Hoover's playbook against Martin Luther King Jr. and then Hampton.

segments.heading: Shaka King on ‘Judas and the Black Messiah’

films.title: Judas and the Black Messiah
films.note: How it came together: the Lucas Brothers pitched King on 'The Conformist' meets 'The Departed' inside COINTELPRO. King brought in writer Will Berson to combine drafts, Ryan Coogler and Charles King's Macro came on to produce, and the cast (Kaluuya, Stanfield, Fishback, Plemons) was attached before the studio deal closed. Fred Hampton Jr. and Akua Njeri signed on as cultural consultants during the second week of filming.

films.title: Newlyweeds
films.note: King's debut feature, which premiered at Sundance the same year as Ryan Coogler's 'Fruitvale Station,' where the two met.

films.title: Lovers Rock
films.note: The 'Small Axe' installment King calls the last thing that blew him away, for capturing what loving music feels like without being a musical.
